Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Score v2 — signal-type-aware scoring runs alongside v1

Bounce/dip-buy signals (MA bounce, PDL reclaim, etc.) were penalised
by v1's trend-following factors. v2 recognises that below-MA and
below-VWAP is *expected* for mean-reversion setups and scores them
fairly. Both scores stored side-by-side; SCORE_VERSION=1 (default)
keeps v1 active for Telegram gating. Also fixes Paper Trading page
style.format crash with na_rep.

commit.message:
Implement pensa daemon sharing via sandbox proxy

Instead of auto-starting a local daemon inside Docker sandboxes (which
creates stale/empty DBs), route sandbox pn traffic through the HTTP
proxy back to the host daemon.

- pensa client: add .pensa/daemon.url to resolution order (after
  PN_DAEMON, before localhost fallback); force proxy when HTTP_PROXY
  is set and URL comes from daemon.url or PN_DAEMON
- pensa main: treat daemon.url as remote (no auto-start)
- ralph: write daemon.url from daemon.port before sandbox launch,
  configure sandbox network proxy to allow localhost, clean up on
  exit via Drop guard; skip all in --command mode
- Dockerfile: remove PN_DAEMON_HOST= override (no longer needed)
- gitignore: add **/.pensa/daemon.url
- specs: update pensa, ralph, springfield specs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: load processor from local checkpoint when base_model is remote HF repo

Colab shared IPs hit HF 429 rate limits when loading the processor from
'Qwen/Qwen2-VL-2B-Instruct'. The SFT checkpoint is a complete Qwen2-VL
model and ships the identical processor config, so fall back to
checkpoint_path when base_model is not a local directory.

Affects rl/train.py and rl/eval_passk.py (same pattern).
No config changes needed — the fallback is automatic.

commit.message:
perf(socket): optimize chat speed and fix connection stability

- Increase heartbeat tolerance (60s → 120s) to prevent premature disconnects
- Add user cache with 60s TTL for faster sender lookups
- Implement parallel DB operations (10+ sequential → 3 parallel)
- Add immediate ACK pattern - users see "sent" in ~30-50ms vs ~160ms
- Defer non-critical work (conversation updates, tracking) to background
- Fix duplicate notifications - skip push when socket delivery succeeds
- Add detailed disconnect logging with duration and device info

Performance improvement:
- Message ACK latency: ~160ms → ~30-50ms
- Disconnect tolerance: 60s → 120s
- No frontend changes required

commit.message:
JEF-99 JEF-80 JEF-81 JEF-82: UI polish — favicon, design tokens, font, colorblind accessibility (#15)

* JEF-99: feat: update favicon and site title for pokemon_stats site

Replace generic React App title/favicon with Pokemon-branded assets:
- Title set to "Pokemon Stats"
- Pokeball SVG favicon added as primary icon with .ico fallback
- Meta description and theme color updated to match Pokemon branding
- Manifest app name and theme color updated

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* JEF-80 JEF-81: refactor: replace hardcoded hex colors with Tailwind design tokens

Define a `pokemon` color palette in tailwind.config.js and a `src/colors.js`
constants file for inline-style usage. Replace all arbitrary [#hex] Tailwind
classes across App.js, PokemonDashboard, PokemonComparison, PokemonCard, and
PokemonSelect with semantic token classes (e.g. text-pokemon-dark, bg-pokemon-red).

Also removes Arial font-family overrides in Dashboard and Comparison, letting
the system font stack from index.css apply globally (JEF-81).

Fixes the radar stroke bug in PokemonDashboard (#4393c3 → COLORS.blue).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* JEF-82: feat: add directional symbols to stat difference column

Replace color-only signaling in the comparison table diff column with
directional symbols so colorblind users have a non-color indicator:
- Positive: ▲ +12 (+5.3%)
- Negative: ▼ -8 (-3.1%)
- Neutral:  = 0

Also fixes the total row not handling the zero-diff case (was always
showing red instead of neutral).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* JEF-99: fix: remove alternate favicon link that caused stale icon display

The rel="alternate icon" fallback for .ico was preventing the SVG favicon
from loading correctly in some browsers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
feat: Shift+Enter inserts newline in terminal (#463)

## Summary
- Shift+Enter and Ctrl+Enter in the terminal now insert a newline
instead of executing the command
- Matches behavior of VS Code integrated terminal, PowerShell, Windows
Terminal, and web apps like Jupyter/Slack

## Changes
- **ShellTerminal.tsx**: Added `attachCustomKeyEventHandler` on the
xterm.js Terminal instance to intercept Shift+Enter and Ctrl+Enter
keydown events. Instead of letting xterm emit `\r` (which executes),
writes `\n` directly to the PTY for shell line continuation.
- **ShellTerminal.test.tsx**: Added 6 test cases covering Shift+Enter,
Ctrl+Enter, plain Enter passthrough, non-Enter key passthrough, and
keyup event ignoring.

## Test Plan
- [x] Shift+Enter writes `\n` to PTY and returns `false` (prevents xterm
default)
- [x] Ctrl+Enter writes `\n` to PTY and returns `false`
- [x] Plain Enter (no modifier) returns `true` (default xterm handling)
- [x] Non-Enter keys with Shift return `true` (unaffected)
- [x] Keyup events for Shift+Enter return `true` (only keydown
intercepted)
- [x] Custom key handler is registered exactly once on mount
- [x] All 5372 existing tests pass
- [x] TypeScript type check passes
- [x] No new lint errors

## Manual Validation
1. Open a terminal in Clubhouse
2. Type a partial command (e.g., `echo "hello`)
3. Press Shift+Enter — should insert a newline without executing
4. Press Ctrl+Enter — same behavior
5. Press Enter — should execute as normal
6. Verify multi-line commands work in zsh, bash, and fish

Closes #448

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resolve 23 of 25 npm security advisories

- jsPDF 4.0.0→4.2.0 (7 PDF injection/XSS/DoS fixes)
- node-quickbooks 2.0.47→2.0.48 (replaced deprecated request with axios)
- DOMPurify, fast-xml-parser, lodash, minimatch, ajv, qs patched
- Removed 39 orphaned packages (leaflet, request dependency tree)

2 remaining: underscore via node-quickbooks (accepted risk, no upstream fix)
